title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to modify the railroad track maintenance credit.
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to a tax credit that helps railroad companies pay for fixing their tracks. Right now, companies can get up to $3,500 per mile of track they own or lease, but this bill increases that amount to $6,100 per mile. Starting in 2026, this credit amount will go up each year to keep up with inflation. The bill also lets companies use this tax credit for tracks they got as late as 2024, instead of only tracks they had before 2015. These changes will start for taxes filed in 2025.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Modification of Railroad Track Maintenance Credit
sections.text: This section changes the tax credit that railroad companies can claim for maintaining their tracks. First, it increases the credit amount from three thousand five hundred dollars to six thousand one hundred dollars per mile of track maintained. Second, it adds a rule that starting in tax year twenty twenty six, this six thousand one hundred dollar amount will go up each year based on inflation. The inflation adjustment will use the same method the tax code uses for other amounts, comparing costs to the year twenty twenty four. Any increase that is not an even multiple of one hundred dollars will be rounded to the nearest hundred dollars. Third, it updates the date that determines which track maintenance costs qualify for the credit from January first two thousand fifteen to January first two thousand twenty four. These changes will apply to money spent on track maintenance in tax years that begin after December thirty first two thousand twenty four.
